repo_explanation.title: Release requires approval
repo_explanation.text: High-risk operational behaviors were detected. Approval is required before publishing.
checklist: Is it restricted to maintenance windows or non-prod?
Is there a health-check and automatic rollback on failure?
Is the affected service list explicit and minimal?
Is the target scope (hosts/env) explicitly limited?
Are commands/args fixed or can user input reach the shell?
Is rollback documented (and tested) for all changes?

findings.title: Skills found outside skills/ directory (repo not following convention)
findings.recommendation: Place all skills under skills/** for governance and discovery.

explanations.title: Release requires approval
explanations.text: High-risk operational behaviors were detected. Approval is required before publishing.
checklist: Is it restricted to maintenance windows or non-prod?
Is there a health-check and automatic rollback on failure?
Is the affected service list explicit and minimal?
Is the target scope (hosts/env) explicitly limited?
Are commands/args fixed or can user input reach the shell?
Is rollback documented (and tested) for all changes?

explanations.title: Requires elevated privileges (sudo/admin)
explanations.text: Running as admin increases blast radius for mistakes and injection. A bad command can modify the system globally or leak sensitive data. Evidence: SKILL.md:22-22.
checklist: Is the target scope (hosts/env) explicitly limited?
Are commands/args fixed or can user input reach the shell?
Is rollback documented (and tested) for all changes?

explanations.title: Controls system services (start/stop/restart/reload)
explanations.text: Service control can cause downtime or partial outages. Restart/reload errors can take production traffic down. Evidence: SKILL.md:22-22.
checklist: Is it restricted to maintenance windows or non-prod?
Is there a health-check and automatic rollback on failure?
Is the affected service list explicit and minimal?

explanations.title: Anchor outside skills/ directory
explanations.text: Skill anchor is not under the convention directory skills/**. Discovery and governance are harder; repo convention is broken.
checklist: Is there a reason to keep skills outside skills/?
Can anchors be moved under skills/?
Is the repo convention documented for the team?
